body.title: CLOSED: SummarizerDevice: URL/doc → tiered output (exec/detail/chunks)
body.result: SummarizerDevice: exec/detail/chunks tiers, URL fetch, HTML strip, Starlette HTTP endpoint, SummarizerShim. 21 tests. Commit 57d7f01.
body.description:
Build a SummarizerDevice in agent_datacenter that takes a URL or document and produces tiered output at three depths: exec-summary (1-3 sentences, quick-scan), detail (paragraph, key points), full-chunks (original text segmented for downstream processing). All three tiers are always produced; consumers select the depth they need.

Igor is a depth consumer — he reads for nuance and will typically request detail or full-chunks, not just exec-summary. Igor composes downstream by consuming the bus envelope and depositing to his knowledge graph (that composition step is NOT in this ticket — this ticket is ADC-only pure device). The tiered output model naturally handles both quick-scan (CC/Rack-Minion) and nuanced reading (Igor) via a depth selector on the response.

API: `POST /api/summarize {url: str | content: str, format: 'exec'|'detail'|'chunks'|'all'}` → `{exec: str, detail: str, chunks: list[str]}`.

Gated on T-cc-palace-schema-design — schema needed to know where in palace the device registers its capability.

**Affected files:** devices/summarizer/ (new), devices/summarizer/device.py, devices/summarizer/shim.py, tests/test_summarizer_device.py
**Design rules:** Inherits BaseDevice; no TheIgors imports; pure device — no palace writes (schema not yet set).
**Scope boundary:** ADC device only. Igor composition downstream (graph deposit) is a separate Igor-side ticket. No palace writes in this ticket — just device + API.
**Test plan:** Unit tests for each tier output shape; integration test for URL fetch → tiered output.
